findings.message: Account 'vault' is mutable but not required to sign. Anyone can pass this account.
Account 'state' is mutable but not required to sign. Anyone can pass this account.
Account 'pool' is mutable but not required to sign. Anyone can pass this account.
Account 'vault' is mutable but not required to sign. Anyone can pass this account.
Account 'vault' is mutable with no type constraint — owner not verified. Attacker can substitute any account.
Account 'state' is mutable with no type constraint — owner not verified. Attacker can substitute any account.
Account 'pool' is mutable with no type constraint — owner not verified. Attacker can substitute any account.
Account 'vault' is mutable with no type constraint — owner not verified. Attacker can substitute any account.
Account 'vault' looks like a PDA but has no seed constraints. Attacker may substitute a malicious PDA.
Account 'state' looks like a PDA but has no seed constraints. Attacker may substitute a malicious PDA.
Account 'vault' looks like a PDA but has no seed constraints. Attacker may substitute a malicious PDA.
Account 'externalProgram' looks like a CPI target program but is not a known safe program. Attacker may substitute a malicious program.
Account 'pool' suggests a typed data account but has no type constraint. Wrong account type may be passed silently.
Account 'vault' appears more than once in instruction 'withdraw'. Attacker may pass same account for two different roles.
